# Transcrição: 23. Trait 22 - Great Salespeople have a deep belief that they won't be challenged by economies, competition, or low prices.

- **Fonte:** 23. Trait 22 - Great Salespeople have a deep belief that they won't be challenged by economies, competition, or low prices.
- **Duração:** 01:17
- **Idioma:** en
- **Modelo:** small

---

## [00:00 - 01:17]

Great salespeople have a deep belief about that they cannot and won't be challenged by economies, competition, or lower prices. You get it? The greats are completely disconnected from most people's realities. The greats tie their products and their services, their offer, to some purpose bigger than just a product, a service, or some offer. But that something is truly positively affects people, their customers, in an unbelievable way. These great salespeople, these great business people are sold on their product in a cult-like manner. I mean they're completely fanatics, they're overboard, they're completely obsessed, compulsed, completely saturated with this concept that you must have this product, it is the best product, it is the best value. Look if you won't buy your product, if you won't insist that people buy your product, if you won't insist that your friends, relatives, neighbors, people you know own that product, use that product, and depend on the product or service you're selling, you can't be great. The greats not only sell their products, sell them relentlessly, but look they're gonna be willing to buy their products, own their products because they believe in them that much.
